source.title: Tracking early lung cancer metastatic dissemination in TRACERx using ctDNA.

finding.description: Landmark analyses of plasma samples collected within 120 days after surgery revealed ctDNA detection in 25% of patients, including 49% of all patients who experienced clinical relapse.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA detection in 25% of patients, including 49% of those who relapsed.

source_quote: Landmark analyses of plasma samples collected within 120 days after surgery revealed ctDNA detection in 25% of patients, including 49% of all patients who experienced clinical relapse.

source.title: Tracking early lung cancer metastatic dissemination in TRACERx using ctDNA.

finding.description: In 102/108 patients evaluable for survival analyses, landmark-positive patients exhibited a HR of 5.3 for OS and an HR of 6.8 for FFR relative to landmark negative patients.
finding.endpoint: overall survival and freedom from recurrence
finding.effect_summary: Landmark-positive patients had a HR of 5.3 for OS and 6.8 for FFR.

source_quote: In 102/108 patients evaluable for survival analyses, landmark-positive patients exhibited a HR of 5.3 (95% CI 2.9 to 9.7, P=1e-09 log-rank test) for OS and an HR of 6.8 for FFR (95% CI 3.7 to 12.3, P=6e-13 log-rank test) relative to landmark negative patients.

source.title: Tracking early lung cancer metastatic dissemination in TRACERx using ctDNA.

finding.description: Sensitivity for variant DNA detection using a 50-variant PSP at 0.01% variant DNA level was >90% at DNA inputs of 20ng and above.
finding.effect_summary: Sensitivity >90% for variant DNA detection at 0.01% level with 20ng DNA input.

source_quote: Sensitivity for variant DNA detection using a 50-variant PSP at 0.01% variant DNA level (representative of ctDNA levels encountered post-resection of NSCLC, using current MRD assays) was >90% at DNA inputs of 20ng and above.

source.title: Tracking early lung cancer metastatic dissemination in TRACERx using ctDNA.

finding.description: Preoperative ctDNA status was associated with overall survival in adenocarcinoma patients (log-rank P=5e-06).
finding.effect_summary: Preoperative ctDNA status associated with OS in adenocarcinoma patients.

source_quote: In patients with adenocarcinoma, ctDNA status was associated with overall survival (OS) (log-rank P=5e-06, Figure 1c).

source.title: Tracking early lung cancer metastatic dissemination in TRACERx using ctDNA.

finding.description: Postoperative ctDNA detection forecasted impending NSCLC relapse, with 25% of patients being landmark MRD positive and 93% of these patients relapsed a median of 228 days post-ctDNA detection.
finding.effect_summary: Postoperative ctDNA detection predicted NSCLC relapse with high PPV.

source_quote: Postoperative ctDNA detection forecasted impending NSCLC relapse, agreeing with prior findings. Here, 25% of patients were landmark MRD positive and 93% of these patients relapsed a median of 228 days post-ctDNA detection.
